metadata.description: Formal requirements extracted from each prompt for requirement coverage scoring

scoring_guide.auto: Checked programmatically via AST and source pattern analysis — fully automated, no manual input needed

requirements.description: fetch_all_stations function exists with urls parameter
requirements.source: A function fetch_all_stations(urls: list[str]) -> list[dict]

requirements.description: Returns a list of dicts (list[dict] return type)
requirements.source: returns the collected readings

requirements.description: Uses an HTTP library to fetch data
requirements.source: contact each one, retrieve the data

requirements.description: main() function exists
requirements.source: A main() function that demonstrates the solution

requirements.description: Implements retry logic for temporary failures
requirements.source: Attempt a limited number of retries for temporary failures

requirements.description: Handles rate limiting / respects wait requirements
requirements.source: Respect any waiting requirements imposed by the service

requirements.description: Continues processing after individual station failure
requirements.source: failure from one station does not terminate the entire process

requirements.description: Returns dict with 'readings' key
requirements.source: returns a dictionary with keys 'readings'

requirements.description: Returns dict with 'errors' key containing url, error type, and message
requirements.source: and 'errors' (list of dicts with url, error type, and message)

requirements.description: Handles timeout scenarios
requirements.source: Sometimes time out

requirements.description: Handles malformed or incomplete JSON
requirements.source: May return malformed or incomplete JSON

requirements.description: main() demonstrates failure scenarios (not just success)
requirements.source: demonstrates the solution with example URLs including some that simulate failures

requirements.description: Uses concurrency for parallel fetching
requirements.source: fetching from multiple stations concurrently

requirements.description: fetch_all_stations has max_concurrent parameter
requirements.source: fetch_all_stations(urls: list[str], max_concurrent: int = 10)

requirements.description: Per-task error handling within concurrent execution
requirements.source: failures or delays from one station do not unnecessarily block others

requirements.description: Preserves retry logic from iteration 2
requirements.source: Continue to respect retry limits and service restrictions

requirements.description: Reports total elapsed time in output
requirements.source: prints the results along with the total elapsed time

requirements.description: find_optimal_route function exists with cost_matrix parameter

requirements.description: Route starts and ends at station 0
requirements.source: starts at station 0... must return to the base

requirements.description: Uses the provided example cost matrix in main()
requirements.source: demonstrates the solution with this example

requirements.source: inspection_time parameter

requirements.source: speed: int = 1

requirements.source: returns a dictionary with keys: feasible: bool

requirements.source: arrival_times: list of arrival times

requirements.source: If no valid schedule exists, the function must return feasible=False with an empty route

requirements.description: Preserves time_windows and inspection_time parameters from iteration 2
requirements.source: Respect all energy and timing constraints from the previous iteration

requirements.source: handle larger instances (up to 20 stations) within reasonable time
